title: Visualizing Distance Between Two Sets in a Metric Space
description: A diagram showing two disjoint sets A and B in a plane, with points a in A and b in B that realize the minimal distance d(A,B) between the sets.
elements: Point a (in Set A, on the boundary closest to B)
Point b (in Set B, on the boundary closest to A)
Distance d(a,b) (line with double arrowhead connecting a and b)
Metric Space X (the containing plane, indicated by a border)
relationships: Sets A and B are disjoint subsets of the metric space X
Point a is an element of set A
Point b is an element of set B
The distance d(A,B) is defined as the infimum of distances d(x,y) for all x in A, y in B
The line d(a,b) represents the minimal distance between the two sets, where d(a,b) = d(A,B)
